diagrams.title: TCP Server Connection Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the system call sequence for establishing TCP client-server connection
elements: accept() returns to server with connected socket
Server: fork() → child handles client → parent loops back
Client: connect() returns after handshake complete
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete TCP connection establishment and server concurrency mechanism

diagrams.title: Zombie Process Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how zombie processes are created and prevented
elements: Child fully reaped, removed from table
diagrams.purpose: Understand zombie formation and prevention strategies in concurrent servers

diagrams.title: Socket Address Structure
diagrams.description: IPv4 socket address structure for bind() and connect()
elements: struct sockaddr_in: sin_family (AF_INET = 2), sin_port (16-bit, network order), sin_addr (32-bit IP), sin_zero[8] (padding)
Conversion functions: htons(), ntohs(), htonl(), ntohl()
Cast to struct sockaddr* for socket functions
diagrams.purpose: Visualize memory layout of socket address structure and byte order conversion requirements
